title: Upload
brief: Don't hand-fly this one — upload a route plan and let Flight Autonomy fly it. Two messages work together: MA_RoutePlanMT is the route itself (the data); MA_MissionPlanActivationCommandMT is the protocol that uploads and activates it (its ActivationCommand field). Walk it in order: (1) PREPARE_FOR_UPLOAD, (2) send the plan + UPLOAD, (3) PREPARE_FOR_ACTIVATION, (4) ACTIVATE. Each FA reply names the next state — click it for the next step — then watch the route run to the loiter.
teaches: A route plan is committed through an ordered handshake, not a single command. Each step is gated on the one before — skip or reorder a step and FA replies a *_FAILED state. Once ACTIVATED, FA flies the legs itself and reports RoutePlanExecutionStatusMT EXECUTING → COMPLETE. This airframe's capability is WAYPOINT_FOLLOWING, not HSA_CSA: a hand-flown MA_FlightCommandMT is REJECTED CAPABILITY_NOT_SUPPORTED — you fly it by uploading a route, not by direct vectors.

objective._doc: route-complete: FA must report RTE-1 COMPLETE (flew the liturgy + the leg) and the vehicle holds the terminal RACETRACK loiter at (-1200,-500). Geometry is turn-friendly for the Mule's 6 deg/tick rate (gentle left turn onto the loiter).
